title: Clarify Windows Rust install path and build tools
description: 00-foundations/04-your-workspace.md:91-98 treats Windows install as a footnote: 'On Windows, download and run the installer from the same site. You may need to install the Visual Studio C++ build tools if prompted. The installer will tell you.' For a novice this is a minefield: the rustup-init.exe prompts with a numbered menu (default installation / customize / cancel), then opens a separate browser tab or installer for the MSVC Build Tools, which itself is a multi-GB download with an installer that shows a component picker most people do not understand (the required component is 'Desktop development with C++'). Without the right components, cargo build fails on every Rust project with linker errors the novice cannot decode. Fix: Add a Windows-specific walkthrough subsection: (1) download rustup-init.exe and run it, (2) when it pauses and says Visual Studio is required, click the link it gives you, (3) in the Visual Studio Installer, check 'Desktop development with C++' and click Install (warn about download size and time), (4) return to rustup-init and press 1 for default install, (5) close and reopen terminal, verify with rustc --version. Include the common error linker message so search engines lead back to the fix. Consider a parallel WSL note for Windows users who want the Linux path instead.
